skill.name: linkedin-automation
skill.description: Guide LinkedIn publishing, profile lookup, company lookup, image upload, comments, and post deletion through Rube MCP.
skill.summary: Automates LinkedIn workflows through Rube MCP and Composio tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown inline code references and tool names, not executable code. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill depends on a third-party MCP endpoint and can publish, comment, upload images, read profile or company data, and delete LinkedIn posts.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint and OAuth-Backed LinkedIn Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and authenticate a LinkedIn connection. This is a real network dependency with access to OAuth-backed LinkedIn actions, so users should understand the third-party trust boundary before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names an external MCP endpoint and an OAuth connection flow. The behavior is intentional and legitimate, but it expands the trust boundary.

medium_findings.title: Automation Can Modify Public LinkedIn Content
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows that create posts, upload images, create comments, and delete posts. These actions can affect a user's public LinkedIn presence and should require clear user confirmation before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences directly include posting, commenting, image upload, and deletion. These are sensitive account actions even when authorized.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tool References Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static command-execution alerts are false positives. The flagged lines contain Markdown inline code spans or fenced examples for Rube and LinkedIn tool names, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown documentation and the flagged backticks delimit code-formatted text. No executable script, interpreter call, or command-substitution context is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to hashing, encryption, or signature verification logic. The file contains documentation text only, and no cryptographic API use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe LinkedIn automation and media metadata, not cryptographic algorithms. No code path performs MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are LinkedIn Identifier Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The flagged lines discuss LinkedIn post IDs, actor URNs, organization IDs, and image IDs needed for API operations, not host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is LinkedIn API identifier handling. No local system, process, network interface, credential store, or environment enumeration is requested.

content.value_statement: LinkedIn posting and profile tasks often require repeated API steps and exact identifiers. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for authorized LinkedIn actions.
actual_capabilities: Searches current Rube MCP tool schemas before LinkedIn actions.
Guides OAuth connection checks for the LinkedIn toolkit.
Creates LinkedIn text posts with explicit visibility settings.
Registers image uploads and attaches image assets to posts.
Retrieves authenticated profile and company information.
Creates comments and deletes authorized LinkedIn posts.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and LinkedIn OAuth connection.
Cannot bypass LinkedIn API scopes, rate limits, or permissions.
Does not look up arbitrary LinkedIn users through profile search.
Requires exact LinkedIn URNs or organization IDs for many actions.

use_cases.title: Publish Scheduled Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and publish LinkedIn posts through an authorized account with explicit visibility settings.

use_cases.title: Manage Company Page Content
use_cases.description: Use organization identifiers to post, verify media assets, and remove outdated content when authorized.

use_cases.title: Prototype LinkedIn API Workflows
use_cases.description: Test Rube MCP LinkedIn tool sequences before adding them to a larger automation process.

prompt_templates.title: Check Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether Rube MCP is available and confirm my LinkedIn toolkit connection status before taking any LinkedIn action.

prompt_templates.title: Create Text Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a LinkedIn post from this draft. Use PUBLIC visibility only after showing me the final text for approval.

prompt_templates.title: Attach Image to Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Register an image upload for my LinkedIn account, upload the image, verify the asset status, and prepare the post with the image attached.

prompt_templates.title: Company Page Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this organization ID to prepare a company page post, confirm the organization URN, attach the approved image asset, and publish only after confirmation.

output_examples.input: Publish this update to LinkedIn after checking my connection.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the Rube MCP LinkedIn connection, retrieves the authenticated profile, confirms the final post text, then publishes with the selected visibility.

output_examples.input: Add this image to a LinkedIn company page announcement.
output_examples.output: The assistant registers the upload, explains the asset status, prepares the announcement, and asks for approval before publishing it to the company page.

output_examples.input: Delete the LinkedIn post with this URN.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the target post identifier and warns that deletion is permanent before using the authorized delete workflow.

best_practices: Always search Rube MCP tools first because schemas can change.
Confirm post text, visibility, actor, and media before publishing.
Respect LinkedIn rate limits and use backoff after 429 responses.
anti_patterns: Do not publish, comment, or delete without explicit user approval.
Do not reuse partial IDs when LinkedIn requires full URNs.
Do not assume OAuth scopes allow company page actions.

faq.question: Does this skill require a LinkedIn account?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires an authenticated LinkedIn connection through Rube MCP.

faq.question: Can it post to a company page?
faq.answer: Yes, if the authenticated user has the required organization permissions and identifiers.

faq.question: Can it upload images?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the register, upload, verify, and attach flow for image posts.

faq.question: Can it search for other LinkedIn users?
faq.answer: No. The documented profile lookup returns the authenticated user's information.

faq.question: Does it bypass LinkedIn limits?
faq.answer: No. LinkedIn API rate limits, scopes, and content restrictions still apply.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a warning because it uses a third-party MCP endpoint and can modify LinkedIn content.
